brief_document.title: Conformance suite run and persistence-across-reopen tests
brief_document.purpose: Prove LibSqlStore is correct by running the shared AC-007 behavioural conformance suite against it, and prove it is durable — the one thing the in-memory oracle cannot demonstrate — by reopening a closed database and asserting state survived. These tests are the cluster's correctness gate.
brief_document.task: Add a conformance test that invokes the AC-007 run_event_store_suite with a factory yielding a fresh LibSqlStore over a temporary local file, run as a real (non-gated) test. Add persistence tests that append history and schedule timers, drop the store, reopen the same file, and assert read_history, list_active, and expired_timers all reflect the persisted state. These exercise the embedded path and need no external service.

requirements.title: Run the AC-007 conformance suite against LibSqlStore
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL run the shared aion-store conformance suite (run_event_store_suite) against LibSqlStore over a temporary local file, as a real runtime test that is not gated or ignored, and the suite SHALL pass.
acceptance: tests/conformance.rs invokes run_event_store_suite (imported from aion-store's test-support surface) with an async factory closure that creates a fresh LibSqlStore over a unique tempfile path per invocation and returns it as Arc<dyn EventStore>.
The test runs under cargo test with no env gating (embedded mode needs no external service) and passes.
Each suite invocation gets a fresh database (a new tempfile), so scenarios do not contaminate each other.
The test is NOT marked #[ignore] and uses no #[allow]/#[expect].

requirements.title: Persistence across close-and-reopen
requirements.spec: WHEN a store is closed (dropped) and reopened against the same file, THE SYSTEM SHALL return the previously appended history, the previously active workflows, and the previously scheduled timers unchanged.
acceptance: tests/persistence.rs appends a multi-event history for one or more workflows and schedules one or more timers, then drops the LibSqlStore.

A new LibSqlStore is opened against the same file path; read_history returns the same events in the same order, list_active returns the same active workflows, and expired_timers returns the previously scheduled timers due as of an appropriate instant.
A workflow that was terminal before reopen is still absent from list_active after reopen (status projection survives because the events do).
The test asserts byte-for-byte event equality across the reopen, proving the blobs round-tripped through durable storage.

requirements.title: Sequence guard survives reopen
requirements.spec: WHEN a store is reopened against a file with existing history, THE SYSTEM SHALL compute the head sequence from the persisted events so that the expected-sequence guard continues correctly after a restart.
acceptance: tests/persistence.rs appends a batch, drops the store, reopens, and then appends a further batch with the correct expected_seq (derived from the persisted head) and asserts it succeeds.
The same reopened store rejects an append with a stale expected_seq with SequenceConflict, proving the head was reconstructed from durable rows rather than reset to zero.
read_history after the second append shows the full combined history in contiguous seq order across the reopen boundary.

boundaries: SHALL NOT modify the AC-007 conformance suite — it is consumed unchanged; if LibSqlStore fails it, the bug is in this crate.
SHALL NOT test embedded-replica sync — that is AS-008 (these tests are embedded local-file only and need no service).
SHALL NOT gate the conformance or persistence tests behind an env var or #[ignore] — embedded mode needs no external service.
SHALL NOT add backend behaviour — these are verification briefs only.
verification: cargo test -p aion-store-libsql passes, including the full conformance run and the persistence tests, with no test skipped.
A reviewer confirms the conformance test imports the real AC-007 suite (not a reimplementation) and that no persistence/conformance test is #[ignore]d or env-gated.
No #[allow], #[expect], or #[ignore] directives in new code.

checklist.text: The shared AC-007 conformance suite (run_event_store_suite) runs against LibSqlStore over a temporary local file as a real, non-gated runtime test and passes.
Persistence-across-reopen tests append history, drop the store, reopen the same file, and assert read_history, list_active, and timers are intact.

stories.text: As the engine, I want a workflow's complete history to survive process restarts so that I can replay it from durable storage after a crash or reboot.
As a store author, I want this backend to pass the shared behavioural conformance suite unmodified so that I have objective proof it matches the in-memory oracle exactly.

constraints.text: unsafe_code = deny. No unsafe in the crate.
No #[allow]/#[expect]/#[ignore] bypasses per CLAUDE.md. Tests needing a remote primary use a runtime env-gate, not #[ignore].
lib.rs is declarations and re-exports only; logic lives in named modules.
500-line file limit (excluding tests/comments/whitespace). The EventStore impl is split across append, read, and timer modules.
Depends only on aion-store (and transitively aion-core) among Aion crates — no dependency on the engine, beamr, or any other backend. Structural; must hold.
Raw SQL is permitted here because this crate IS a storage backend (the storage layer). No other crate issues SQL against this store; they go through the EventStore trait.
WorkflowStatus is derived from event history via aion-core's projection. No mutable stored status field; a cached projection must never diverge from the events.
LibSqlStore must pass the shared AC-007 conformance suite unmodified — the same behavioural standard as the oracle.
append's sequence guard and event inserts execute in a single libSQL transaction; a mismatch writes nothing and returns SequenceConflict — no partial batch is ever persisted.
LibSqlConfig is Deserialize-only and carries no hardcoded durability/WAL defaults; tunable values are surfaced for the operator to set, per CLAUDE.md no-assumed-defaults.
All libsql and serde errors are mapped into the StoreError taxonomy at the crate boundary; no foreign error type leaks across the EventStore surface.

resolved_context.intention: When an engine operator embeds Aion and writes no storage configuration, this is the store they get: a single file on disk, zero external infrastructure, full durability. The same crate serves the distributed path — the local file becomes an embedded replica syncing to a remote primary — without the operator learning a second backend. The in-memory reference store (cluster AC) proves the contract but forgets on restart; LibSqlStore makes durability the easy default, held to the identical behavioural standard as the oracle. A workflow that sleeps for months and resumes needs its history to survive every crash and reboot in between. When this cluster is done, an operator writes LibSqlStore::open("app.db") and has a production-grade durable event store the engine cannot tell apart from the oracle except that it remembers; the shared AC-007 conformance suite passes against it unchanged, plus a durability-specific suite proving state survives close-and-reopen.
